recalls.Description: FENGQS has announced a recall of its F7 Pro electric bikes due to potential fire hazards associated with the lithium-ion battery. These e-bikes, identifiable by the blue color and the model number 'F7 Pro' printed on the chain stay, were manufactured on April 21, 2024. The battery capacity of 21.5Ah and the production date can be found on a label attached to the front of the battery. Consumers are urged to take this recall seriously to avoid potential injuries.
recalls.Title: FENGQS F7 Pro Electric Bikes Recalled Due to Fire Risks; Serious Injury or Death Possible
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact FENGQS at 607-307-2127 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, email recall@fengqs.work, or visit www.fengqs.com/pages/recalls or www.fengqs.com and click on 'Recalls' at the top.

Images.Caption: The battery's capacity (21.5Ah) and the production date (2024/4/21) are printed on a label on the front of the battery

Injuries.Name: FENGQS has received 13 reports of e-bikes igniting, two resulting in fires with property damage totaling $4,000.

Manufacturers.Name: Shenzhen Fengqisi Car Industry Co., Ltd., dba FENGQS, of China

Retailers.Name: Available on Amazon between May 2024 and December 2024, priced between $700 and $1,200.

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire and burns due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using the e-bikes immediately and request a full refund from FENGQS. Mark the battery's production date with 'recalled' and submit a photo to recall@fengqs.work. Dispose of the battery according to local regulations.

recalls.metaDescription: FENGQS recalls F7 Pro electric bikes due to fire hazards. Immediate action required for consumer safety. Contact for refunds and disposal instructions.

recalls.Description: KTM is recalling off-road motorcycles from model years 2022 to 2025 due to a potential crash hazard. The affected GASGAS motorcycles are red with a white GASGAS logo, while the Husqvarna models feature a combination of white, blue, and yellow with the Husqvarna logo. The vehicle identification number (VIN) can be found on the right side of the steering head, with specific letters indicating the model year: N for 2022, P for 2023, R for 2024, and S for 2025. A detailed list of affected models is provided below.
recalls.Title: KTM Recalls Off-Road Motorcycles Due to Crash Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact GASGAS and Husqvarna Motorcycles at 888-985-6090 from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. You can also visit https://www.gasgas.com/en-us.html or https://www.husqvarna-motorcycles.com/en-us.html and click on 'Safety Recall Information' at the bottom of the page. To check if your motorcycle is affected, visit https://www.husqvarna-motorcycles.com/en-us/service/Service-and-safety-check.html or https://www.gasgas.com/en-us/service/service-and-safety-check.html.

Retailers.Name: Authorized GASGAS and Husqvarna dealers across the country from January 2022 through August 2024, priced between $10,000 and $13,000.

Importers.Name: KTM North America Inc., of Amherst, Ohio

Hazards.Name: Cracked front brake caliper reducing braking effectiveness, leading to crash risk.

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled motorcycles immediately and contact an authorized dealer for a free inspection and repair. An authorized dealer will assess the front brake caliper for defects. KTM/GASGAS and Husqvarna Mobility will reach out to all known purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: KTM recalls off-road motorcycles from 2022-2025 due to crash hazards. Check if your motorcycle is affected and learn how to get it inspected.

recalls.Description: Santa Cruz Bicycles has announced a recall for their Heckler 9 electric bicycles, which are equipped with a 720W lithium-ion battery housed in a black case beneath the frame. The bicycles feature 'SANTA CRUZ' printed on the downtube and 'Heckler' on the top tube and inside the left chainstay. Available in multiple colors, including Gloss Avocado Green and Maritime Gray, these bikes pose a significant fire hazard due to the potential overheating of the battery. Proper disposal of the recalled battery is crucial, as it cannot be discarded in regular trash or recycling bins. Instead, consumers should check with their local household hazardous waste collection center for proper disposal options.
recalls.Title: Santa Cruz Bicycles Issues Recall for Heckler 9 Electric Bicycles Due to Fire Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, consumers can reach Santa Cruz Bicycles at 833-944-8335 from 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or visit https://santacruzbicycles.com/pages/recall-heckler-9-lithium-ion-battery for more details.

Products.Name: Heckler 9 Electric Bicycle
Products.NumberOfUnits: About 2,850 (In addition, about 550 were sold in Canada)

Images.Caption: Recalled Santa Cruz Bicycles Heckler 9 Electric Bicycle
Recalled Santa Cruz Bicycles Heckler 9 Electric Bicycle serial number on battery pack

Injuries.Name: The firm has received three reports of fires, including some property damage. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Santa Cruz dealers across the nation from October 2021 to January 2025, priced between $8,750 and $13,900.

Distributors.Name: Santa Cruz Bicycles LLC, of Santa Cruz, California

Hazards.Name: Risk of fire due to overheating lithium-ion battery

Remedies.Name: Stop using the bicycle, disconnect the battery, and register online for a free replacement battery.

recalls.metaDescription: Santa Cruz recalls Heckler 9 electric bicycles due to fire hazards. Learn how to check if your bike is affected and get a free replacement battery.

recalls.Description: Kawasaki Motors USA has announced a recall for its 2025 KX450, KX450SR, and KX450X motorcycles, which are designed for competition and cross-country use. These two-wheel motorcycles are available in a distinctive lime green color. The recall affects specific model numbers and vehicle identification numbers (VINs) that can be found on the right side of the vehicle and frame head pipe, respectively.
recalls.Title: Kawasaki Motors USA Issues Recall for 2025 KX450, KX450SR, and KX450X Motorcycles Due to Potential Crash Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Kawasaki Motors USA at 866-802-9381 from 7 a.m. to 4 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday. Additional information is available online at https://www.kawasaki.com/en-us/owner-center/recalls or by visiting www.kawasaki.com and selecting 'Recalls' at the bottom of the page.

Retailers.Name: Available at authorized Kawasaki dealers across the nation from June 2024 to March 2025, priced between $10,500 and $13,600.

Importers.Name: Kawasaki Motors Corp. U.S.A., of California

Hazards.Name: Risk of crash due to potential failure of the primary gear for the crank shaft.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease using the recalled motorcycles and contact an authorized Kawasaki dealer for a complimentary repair, which involves replacing the primary gear.

recalls.metaDescription: Kawasaki recalls 2025 KX450, KX450SR, and KX450X motorcycles due to crash hazards. Contact Kawasaki for repairs.

recalls.Description: Triumph Motorcycles America has issued a recall for the 2024 and 2025 model years of the TF250-X closed course competition motorcycles. These motorcycles are identifiable by their black color and vehicle identification numbers (VIN) ranging from BP6028 to CC7868, located on the left side of the steering head. Owners can verify if their VIN is part of this recall by visiting https://www.triumphmotorcycles.com/owners/recalls.
recalls.Title: Recall of Triumph TF250-X Off-Road Motorcycles Due to Potential Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach Triumph Motorcycles at 888-284-6288 from 8:30 a.m. to 6 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday, via email at tma.aftersales@triumphmotorcycles.com, or by visiting https://www.triumphmotorcycles.com/owners/recalls for further details.

Products.Name: Triumph TF250-X Closed Course Competition Motorcycles

Images.Caption: Recalled Triumph TF250-X Closed Course Competition Motorcycle

Injuries.Name: The firm has received 16 incident reports of the switch cube cracking. Seven of those resulting in the motorcycle starting unexpectedly. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available at Triumph dealers across the country from July 2023 to December 2024, priced around $9,500.

Importers.Name: Triumph Motorcycles America Ltd., of Atlanta, Georgia

Hazards.Name: Risk of unexpected motorcycle start due to cracking of the right-hand plastic switch cube when exposed to brake fluid.

Remedies.Name: Consumers are advised to stop using the recalled motorcycles immediately, disconnect the battery, and contact an authorized Triumph Motorcycle dealer for a complimentary repair, which includes replacing the right-hand switch cube. Triumph Motorcycles America will reach out to all known purchasers directly.

recalls.metaDescription: Triumph recalls TF250-X motorcycles due to injury risks. Check your VIN and contact Triumph for a free repair.

recalls.Description: American Honda Motor is recalling certain off-road motorcycles from the 2023-2024 model years, specifically the CRF50F, CRF110F, and CRF125F. The CRF50F and CRF110F are available in red or white, while the CRF125F comes in red. Each motorcycle features the "HONDA" logo on the seat and has its model number located on the frame above the engine. The vehicle identification number (VIN) can be found on the left side of the steering head, allowing consumers to verify if their motorcycle is affected by this recall through Honda Powersports & Motorcycle Recalls.
recalls.Title: Honda Recalls 2023-2024 Off-Road Motorcycles Due to Potential Crash Risks
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach American Honda Motor at 866-784-1870, available Monday to Friday from 9 a.m. to 7:30 p.m. PT, or visit https://powersports.honda.com/recalls or https://powersports.honda.com/contact-us and select "Recalls" at the page's bottom.

Images.Caption: Recalled Honda Off-Road Motorcycle Model CRF50F
Recalled Honda Off-Road Motorcycle Model CRF110F
Recalled Honda Off-Road Motorcycle Model CRF125F

Injuries.Name: The firm has received one report of a crash, resulting in a knee injury.

Retailers.Name: Available at Honda dealers across the nation from September 2022 to May 2024, priced between $1,700 and $3,900.

Importers.Name: American Honda Motor Co. Inc., of Torrance, California

Hazards.Name: Detachment of the grip from the handlebar, leading to crash and injury risks.

Remedies.Name: Consumers must cease use of the affected motorcycles immediately and contact American Honda Motor to arrange a complimentary inspection and repair. Honda is proactively reaching out to all known buyers.

recalls.metaDescription: Honda recalls 2023-2024 off-road motorcycles due to crash risks. Check if your motorcycle is affected and learn how to get it repaired.
